name: I.M. Detroit District
description_short: IM Detroit District Credit Union is a Detroit-based credit union serving 800+ members since 1957, offering auto loans and standard banking services from two branch locations.
description_long:
IM Detroit District Credit Union was established in 1957 and has operated for over 65 years as a member-owned financial institution in Detroit, Michigan. The credit union maintains a modest asset base of $394,000 and serves a membership of more than 800 individuals throughout the Detroit area. As a credit union, it operates on a non-profit cooperative model where members are both customers and partial owners of the institution.

The credit union offers auto loan financing as its primary lending product, along with standard banking services typical of credit union membership. Members can access banking services through two physical branch locations in Detroit: the head office at 2101 Gratiot Ave and a secondary branch at 465 Clairpointe Woods Dr. The institution provides phone-based customer service and maintains an online presence for member inquiries and account management.

IM Detroit District distinguishes itself as a long-established community credit union with deep roots in Detroit dating back nearly seven decades. The organization's longevity and local presence in a specific geographic area reflect its commitment to serving a defined membership base rather than pursuing rapid growth or expansion. The credit union's focus on auto lending suggests specialization in helping members finance vehicle purchases, a core credit union lending function.

As a small credit union with $394,000 in assets, IM Detroit District operates at a significantly smaller scale than larger financial institutions, which may limit the breadth of services, technology capabilities, and product offerings available to members. Prospective members should verify current service availability and product terms, as small institutions sometimes have limited online banking features or restricted lending criteria. The modest asset size means fewer ATM locations and potentially limited competitive rates compared to larger regional credit unions. Many banks offer installment loans with fixed monthly payments, giving borrowers a clear payoff timeline.
pros: Established credit union with 65+ years of history serving Detroit community since 1957
Member-owned cooperative structure means profits benefit members rather than external shareholders
Two physical branch locations in Detroit for in-person member service and transactions
Auto loan financing available for vehicle purchases and refinancing
Phone-based customer service available at (313) 259-5817 for member inquiries
Non-profit credit union model typically offers lower fees than commercial banks
Direct member contact option through website for personalized assistance
cons: Very small asset base of $394,000 limits product offerings and technological capabilities compared to larger institutions
Limited membership base of 800+ members may result in restricted lending criteria or loan amounts
Only two branch locations in Detroit restricts physical access for members outside service area
Limited service details provided on website suggests potentially narrow product range beyond auto loans
Small institution size may mean fewer ATM partnerships and limited online banking features
best_for: Detroit-area residents seeking community-focused credit union membership with local branch access
Individuals looking to finance vehicle purchases through auto loan products
Members prioritizing non-profit financial institution structure over commercial bank alternatives
Long-term Detroit community members seeking established local financial relationships
diagnosis: IM Detroit District Credit Union is best for Detroit-area residents seeking a small, community-based credit union focused on auto lending and basic banking services. The primary caveat is that as a very small institution with only $394,000 in assets and 800 members, it likely offers limited product variety and technological features compared to larger credit unions or banks, making it suitable primarily for established local members rather than those seeking comprehensive financial services.
typical_results_timeline: Membership approval and account opening typically takes 1-3 business days. Loan decisions are usually faster than traditional banks.
